violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; LINE: 151
; console.log(`  SKIP (duplicate): ${issue.title.slice(0, 80)}...`)
; "..." is appended as a string literal regardless of whether slice truncated.
; For titles with length <= 80, no truncation occurred but "..." implies it did.
(declare-const title_len Int)
(declare-const chars_shown Int)
(declare-const truncation_occurred Int)   ; 1=yes, 0=no
; Transition: slice(0,80) on a short title returns the full title
(assert (> title_len 0))
(assert (<= title_len 80))
(assert (= chars_shown title_len))
(assert (= truncation_occurred 0))
; Transition: "..." is a hardcoded literal in the template string — always appended
; Violation: log visually implies truncation (truncation_occurred=0 but "..." present)
(assert (= truncation_occurred 0))
(assert (not (= truncation_occurred 1)))
(check-sat)
; sat → title_len=25: log shows "25-char-title..." implying truncation that did not occur
